creator: philip@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2006-10-31 21:51:14 (+0000), Andrew Turner <andrew+ports@fubar.geek.nz> wrote:
> Update the devel/lua50-posix port to support both lua 5.0 and 5.1.

The 5.0 bits of this seem to work, but the new lua-posix port (5.1) does not
like to compile here (-CURRENT):

 | ===>  Building for lua51-posix-5.0
 | LD_PRELOAD=/usr/local/lib/lua51/liblua.so:/usr/local/lib/lua51/liblualib.so /usr/local/bin/lua51/lua -lposix test.lua
 | /libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Cannot open "/usr/local/lib/lua51/liblua.so"
 | *** Error code 1
 | 
 | Stop in /home/philip/projects/freebsd/ports/devel/lua-posix/work/posix.
 | *** Error code 1
 | 
 | Stop in /home/philip/projects/freebsd/ports/devel/lua-posix.

I don't currently have the brain-bandwidth to hunt this down.  It's probably
something simple. :-)

Any idea?

Thanks!

 - Philip

-- 
Philip Paeps                                    Please don't Cc me, I am
philip@freebsd.org                               subscribed to the list.

  Humans suffering from a conflict of signals aren't the best people
  to be holding guns, especially when they've just witnessed a
  natural childbirth, which definitely looked an un-American way
  of bringing new citizens into the world.
  	-- (Terry Pratchett & Neil Gaiman, Good Omens)
